assumptions: The dividend receivable is a recognised intragroup monetary item denominated in a foreign currency.
The receivable and corresponding payable are between group entities with different functional currencies, so the related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
Any hedge relationship considered would satisfy the formal designation, documentation and hedge effectiveness requirements in IFRS 9 paragraph 6.4.1.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup foreign-currency receivable can be considered under the fair value hedge model because IFRS 9 allows recognised assets or liabilities to be hedged for a particular risk affecting profit or loss, including the intragroup monetary item exception in consolidation.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Cash flow hedge is the other distinct IFRS 9 hedge accounting model available for particular risks associated with recognised assets or liabilities, so it is a relevant candidate model for a foreign-currency receivable if the qualifying criteria are met.
